meaning: n.廓尔喀族人(尼泊尔的主要居民),英国(或印度)军队中的尼泊尔族士兵
examples: The hanging lamps swinging a chain of sound to scare away the former Gurkha this student, causing him a series of thinking.
Despite a huge conventional search operation (followed by more exotic efforts involving psychics and a Gurkha parachuted into Cambodia), no trace was ever found of him.
Gurkha flying in his swift and powerful Vimana hurled against the three cities of the Vrishis and Andhakas a single projectile charged with all the power of the Universe.
In September a specialist help centre for Gurkha veterans and their families opened in Reading.
There are 10, 000 Gurkha veterans living in the UK, many of them in Berkshire and Hampshire.
